title: New Company Registration Form - Canada.ca
content:
## New Company Registration Form
NHP Product Licensing Bulletin No. 3, July 9, 2025
- New applicants are required to register their company and obtain a company code, which is needed to complete the Product Licence Application (PLA) or Site Licence Application (SLA) form.
- The Natural and Non-prescription Health Products Directorate (NNHPD) has developed a New Company Registration Form to ensure requests for a company code are complete and  efficiently processed.
- This form only applies to companies that have not been issued a company code (i.e., new applicants).
- The New Company Registration Form replaces the current process for a “New Company Registration”, outlined in Section 4.7 of the Natural Health Products Management of Applications Policy (NHP MAP) (2019 version). An updated version of the NHP MAP will be published to reflect the new process in Fall 2025.
NNHPD is committed to continuously enhancing its service delivery, including streamlining company registration for new applicants.
This bulletin and process applies to NHP applications (both product and site licensing).
Company registration is only required for companies that have not yet been assigned a company code (i.e., new applicants).
All new applicants must register their company and obtain a company code before submitting their first PLA or SLA. This step is necessary to enable applicants to complete and submit the PLA form and to ensure traceability though the application process.
Companies that previously submitted a PLA or SLA have already been issued a company code and are not required to obtain a new one. Applicants can locate their company code on NNHPD correspondence (e.g., Application acknowledgement letters, Issuance Letters, Information Request Notices (IRNs),  etc.).
The New Company Registration Form is now available for submitting requests to obtain a company code. To ensure optimal service delivery and faster processing, we encourage all new applicants to use this form.
Once the form is submitted, NNHPD will email new applicants, as soon as possible, a unique five-digit company code to use for all future applications.
For inquiries about this bulletin, please contact us by email: nnhpd-dpsnso@hc-sc.gc.ca .
